key_insight: The scratch test is a fast, sensitive way to assess adhesion and failure in thermally sprayed coatings, but its results must be interpreted in the context of coating microstructure, residual stress, and porosity.
engineering_relevance: For aerospace, energy, automotive, and industrial coatings, improved scratch-test interpretation can help predict coating durability and guide process selection and quality control.
method: Review and compare scratch-testing methods used for thermally sprayed coatings
Correlate scratch response with microstructure, residual stress, porosity, and failure modes
Summarize critical-load evaluation tools such as optical microscopy, acoustic emission, and multi-axis force measurements
evidence_signal: The paper synthesizes scratch-test findings across APS, HVOF, HVAF, D-gun, and related coatings, showing that failure interpretation varies significantly with testing methodology and coating architecture.
watch_item: Scratch-test standards are not yet fully consistent, so critical load values can be method-dependent and should not be compared across studies without matching protocols.
paper_title: A critical analysis of the scratch test for thermally sprayed coatings
